channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: In Pacific Northwest, 2 toss-up US House races could determine control of narrowly divided Congress
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — In their battle for Congress, national Republicans and Democrats are keenly eyeing the Pacific Northwest, where two of the most competitive U.S. House races in the country are playing out.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris viewed more positively by Hispanic women than by Hispanic men: AP-NORC poll
item.description: A solid majority of Hispanic women have a positive opinion of Vice President Kamala Harris and a negative view of former President Donald Trump, but Hispanic men are divided on both candidates, according to a recent AP-NORC poll.
item.dc:creator: Fernanda Figueroa And Linley Sanders, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Harris rips Trump for his criticism of federal storm response and calls for 'dignity'
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris and the White House criticized Donald Trump for his attacks on the federal response to Hurricanes Helene and Milton and suggested he was wrongly trying to turn the deadly storms to his political advantage.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA VOLMERT, DARLENE SUPERVILLE and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris calls Trump debate decision a ‘pretty weak move,' praises Native community at Arizona rally
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris returned to the Grand Canyon State for a rally on Thursday, speaking out about Native American governance, Trump's debate decision and Project 2025
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Obama calls out ‘brothers’ apprehensive to vote for Harris: ‘You’re thinking of sitting out?’
item.description: Former President Barack Obama took heat for appearing to admonish Black voters who are apprehensive about supporting Vice President Harris.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's complaints about '60 Minutes' put a spotlight on editing at the nation's top newsmagazine
item.description: Donald Trump skipped a “60 Minutes” interview this past week, but he's attacking CBS for how it handled its session with opponent Kamala Harris, calling it a “giant fake news scam” and suggesting the network was out to protect her.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Utah candidates for Mitt Romney's open US Senate seat debate approaches to climate change
item.description: The Republican and Democratic candidates vying for Mitt Romney’s open U.S. Senate seat are aiming to convince Utah voters that they have the better blueprint to tackle climate change and protect the state’s vast natural resources.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Schoenbaum, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Harris was asked to name a few of Trump's virtues. Here's her response
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris was asked to name three virtues that her opponent in next month's election, Donald Trump, possesses — but was unable to come up with even one.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Obama urges Black men to show up for Harris as he campaigns in critical Pennsylvania
item.description: Former President Barack Obama has given a blistering critique of his White House successor Donald Trump and urged Black men to show up for Kamala Harris as he campaigned in Pittsburgh at the start of a swing-state tour for the Democratic ticket.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tallahassee sends city crews to aid Tampa Bay recovery efforts
item.description: As communities wait for daylight to come Thursday morning to see the damage that has been done and for emergency personnel to start rescue efforts, counties in north Florida are sending their crews to the Tampa Bay area to aid in recovery.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics: Harris and the Hurricanes
item.description: The latest updates from the 2024 campaign trail, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Speculation continues over Cuomo run for NYC mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- There has been growing speculation this week that former Gov. Andrew Cuomo is rapidly approaching a decision about whether or not to run for New York City mayor.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: NJ assemblyman's car stolen, house broken into
item.description: HAZLET, N.J. (PIX11) - Video from the home where a New Jersey assemblyman is currently living shows alleged burglars break into the home and speed away with his personal car - complete with State Assembly plates.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Follow the Money: Understanding FEMA's disaster budget in Hurricane Milton aftermath
item.description: In the event of natural disasters, it can be hard to understand the money, including whether or not FEMA has sufficient funding to respond and if Congress will approve additional relief.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden, NATO head claim a stronger Obama response to Crimea invasion may have prevented Ukraine war
item.description: Outgoing NATO chief Jens Stoltenberg insisted the war in Ukraine may not have happened if the U.S. and NATO had a stronger response to Russia's invasion of Crimea in 2014.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia has suffered more casualties in Ukraine war than all other conflicts combined since WWII: Pentagon
item.description: Russia has suffered some 600,000 casualties in its war with Ukraine — more than its losses in every conflict since World War II combined, according to U.S. officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris faces new urgency to explain how her potential presidency would be different from Biden's
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — With less than four weeks until Election Day, Vice President Kamala Harris is facing new urgency to define how her potential presidency would be different from that of President Joe Biden.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump leads Harris by 4 points in Florida: Survey
item.description: Former President Trump is leading Vice President Harris by a narrow 4 points in Florida, according to a new survey of likely voters in the state.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: VP Harris ripped by victim of illegal immigrant released under her program over resurfaced speech
item.description: VP Kamala Harris, as California DA, said in a speech that an illegal immigrant let out of jail as part of a program she launched and then assaulting a woman was a "glitch.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Walz silent on support for eliminating Electoral College after Harris camp says it doesn't back ban
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz remained silent when pressed on his stance about eliminating the Electoral College, after the Harris campaign said his viewpoint was not an official campaign policy position.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris accepts invite to CNN town hall as another Trump debate appears unlikely
item.description: Vice President Harris has accepted an invitation from CNN to participate in a town hall on the network a week before Election Day, as it appears unlikely that former President Trump will agree to a second debate with her.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Obama-era DACA program heads back to federal appeals court
item.description: Legal wranglings pertaining to DACA program persist with arguments before the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fifth Circuit on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris, Trump in virtual dead heat in major swing states
item.description: Vice President Harris and former President Trump are nearly tied in the major swing states that will play a deciding role in the election, according to polling released Thursday by The Hill and Emerson College Polling.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from the Arizona Senate debate
item.description: Rep. Ruben Gallego (D-Ariz.) and Republican candidate Kari Lake faced off in their only debate in the Arizona Senate race Wednesday, and the two contenders frequently traded barbs. Gallego and Lake, who are vying for retiring Sen.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Some Minneapolis Metro Transit trains will switch to buses starting Oct. 9
item.description: Part of the Metro Transit’s Blue and Green Lines will be closed starting at 10 p.m. on Wednesday for scheduled maintenance. It will reopen by 3 a.m. Monday, Oct. 14.
item.dc:creator: Anika Besst, MPR News

item.title: Election drop boxes open in San Diego County
item.description: While mail ballots are expected to arrive this week for registered voters, 150 official ballot drop boxes opened Tuesday in San Diego County for the November Presidential General Election.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Florida has nearly all ballots counted on Election Day, while California can take weeks. This is why
item.description: Why is it that some states have nearly all the ballots counted within hours of polls closing while others take weeks?
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cook Political Report shifts Wisconsin Senate race toward GOP
item.description: The Cook Political Report on Tuesday shifted the Wisconsin Senate race from "lean Democrat" to "toss up," handing Republican Eric Hovde a boost as he tries to unseat Sen. Tammy Baldwin (D-Wis.).
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation
